description: Figure 6 presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) derived orientation maps and the associated {110} pole figures for selected recrystallized grains in an Fe–8% Al ferritic steel after hot torsion at 1100 °C to a strain of ε = 2 under high strain rates. Panels (a) and (b) correspond to strain rates of 5 s⁻¹ and 10 s⁻¹, respectively. In each panel, the left image displays the microstructure where recrystallized grains with internal misorientations below 2° are highlighted in blue, while the right side shows the corresponding {110} pole figure. The maximum intensities are 1.77 m.r.d. at 5 s⁻¹ and 1.66 m.r.d. at 10 s⁻¹. Both pole figures exhibit very weak, nearly random textures approaching a MacKenzie-type distribution, indicating that discontinuous dynamic recrystallization (DDRX) produces randomly oriented recrystallized grains. This weak texture is advantageous for mitigating surface roping defects during subsequent deep drawing.
